frameworks.name: uncertainty-aware exploration
frameworks.one_line: Choices weigh approaching vs avoiding uncertainty about option values.

frameworks.name: resource-rational analysis
frameworks.one_line: Exploration policy is shaped by cognitive capacity constraints.

frameworks.name: exploration-exploitation tradeoff
frameworks.one_line: Active sampling to reduce goal-relevant uncertainty during learning.

core_question: How do humans reconcile the conflicting tendencies to approach versus avoid uncertainty when exploring?
key_assumptions: Exploration's purpose is to reduce goal-relevant uncertainty
Cognitive capacity for processing uncertainty is limited
Choice between better-known and lesser-known options reflects uncertainty policy
manipulations: Overall uncertainty level about the choice options
Relative uncertainty between better-known vs lesser-known option on each trial
key_findings: Participants generally chose to approach uncertainty (sample lesser-known)
When overall uncertainty was highest, they switched to avoiding uncertainty
Uncertainty-avoidance was associated with faster decisions
Avoiding uncertainty did not impair learning despite lower observed information rate
interpretations: Balancing approach and avoidance of uncertainty reduces cognitive cost of exploration in a resource-rational manner
